payload.text:
# Merge Request

## Source Branch
task/cyan-lane-1-20260503T045512

## Target Branch
orch/cyan-20260503T045512

## Merge Message
merge: wave 1 lane 1 — TP-016

## Tasks Completed
- TP-016: Add `aliases` to commands and subcommands

## File Scope
- `packages/core/src/types.ts` — add `aliases?: readonly string[]` to
- CommandMeta
- `packages/core/src/router.ts` — extend `resolveCommand` to resolve aliases
- to the canonical node; include aliases in the `available` list of
- `COMMAND_NOT_FOUND` errors
- `packages/core/src/crust.ts` — add registration-time collision detection
- in the `.command(name, cb)` and `.command(builder)` paths
- `packages/core/src/validation.ts` — extend `validateCommandTree` to detect
- alias collisions across the subcommand tree (catches plugin-installed
- subcommands that bypass `.command()`)
- `packages/core/src/router.test.ts` — alias resolution tests, collision
- surfacing in `details.available`, canonical-path preservation
- `packages/core/src/crust.test.ts` — builder-level alias tests, eager
- collision throwing, `meta({ aliases: [...] })` plumbing
- `packages/core/src/validation.test.ts` (or wherever validation tests
- live — grep first; co-locate if none exists) — alias collision detection
- via `validateCommandTree`
- `packages/plugins/src/help.ts` — render aliases inline next to canonical
- name in `formatCommandsSection`
- `packages/plugins/src/autocomplete.ts` — include aliases in the
- candidate list passed to `findSuggestions`; the suggested string in the
- output must be the canonical name, not the alias matched against
- `packages/plugins/src/plugins.test.ts` (or `help.test.ts` /
- `autocomplete.test.ts` — grep first to match existing test layout) —
- regression tests for help rendering and did-you-mean alias behavior
- `packages/man/src/mdoc.ts` — render aliases alongside canonical name in
- the SUBCOMMANDS section
- `packages/man/src/mdoc.test.ts` — regression test that aliases appear in
- rendered roff output
- `apps/docs/co…
I'll perform the merge following the task-merger workflow. Let me start by checking the current state.

payload.argsPreview: cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && git status && echo "---" && git branch --show-current && echo "---" && git log --oneline -5

payload.text: I'm on a temp merge branch. Let me check what branches exist and verify the source/target setup.

payload.argsPreview: cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && git branch -a | head -30 && echo "---" && git log --oneline orch/cyan-20260503T045512 -3 2>&1 && echo "---" && git log --oneline task/cyan-lane-1-20260503T045512 -3 2>&1

payload.text: Current HEAD is `_merge-temp-cyan-20260503T045512` which sits on `orch/cyan-...`. I need to merge into the orch branch. Let me checkout target first.

payload.argsPreview: cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && git log --oneline task/cyan-lane-1-20260503T045512 ^orch/cyan-20260503T045512 | head -20

payload.text: Now let me check out the orch branch and perform the merge.

payload.argsPreview: cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && git checkout orch/cyan-20260503T045512 2>&1 && git log --oneline -1
cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && git merge --no-ff task/cyan-lane-1-20260503T045512 -m "merge: wave 1 lane 1 — TP-016" 2>&1

payload.text: Clean merge with no conflicts. Capture the merge commit SHA, then run verification.

payload.argsPreview: cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && git rev-parse HEAD
cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && bun install --frozen-lockfile 2>&1 | tail -20
cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && bun run check 2>&1 | tail -30
cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && bun run check:types 2>&1 | tail -40
cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && bun run test 2>&1 | tail -30

payload.text: All verification passed: Biome check ✓, type check ✓ (21/21), tests ✓ (21/21, FULL TURBO cached). Let me confirm zero failures across the full test output.

payload.argsPreview: cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && bun run test 2>&1 | grep -E "(fail|FAIL|Ran [0-9]+ tests)" | head -30

payload.text: I see two failing tests in `@crustjs/skills`. Let me investigate — these may be pre-existing on the orch branch (unrelated to TP-016).

payload.argsPreview: cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && bun run test 2>&1 | grep -E "@crustjs/skills" | tail -40

cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && bun run test 2>&1 | grep -E "@crustjs/skills:test:.*(fail|Ran [0-9]+ tests|pass [0-9]+|0 pass|0 fail|[0-9]+ fail)" | tail -20

cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && bun run test 2>&1 | grep -E "@crustjs/skills:test" | grep -E "(fail|pass)" | wc -l && cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && bun run test 2>&1 | grep "@crustjs/ski
cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && bun run test 2>&1 | grep -E "@crustjs/skills:test:" | grep -E "Ran|fail$|pass$|2 fail|0 fail" | head
cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && bun run test 2>&1 | awk '/@crustjs\/skills:test:/' | tail -20

cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && bun run test 2>&1 | grep -E "skills:test.*Ran|skills:test.*fail|skills:test.*pass$" | head -10 && echo "---" && cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && bun run test

payload.text: Let me check if these `@crustjs/skills` failures are pre-existing on the orch branch (not introduced by the merge).

payload.argsPreview: cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && git stash 2>&1; git checkout orch/cyan-20260503T045512 -- . 2>&1; echo "---"; git log --oneline HEAD~1 -1 && echo "---running test on pre-merge HEAD---" && git checkout HEAD~1 2>&1 | head -3
cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && git log --oneline HEAD -1 && cd packages/skills && bun test 2>&1 | tail -15
cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge/packages/skills && bun test 2>&1 | grep -E "(fail|pass|Ran [0-9]+)" | grep -E "fail|Ran " | head -10

payload.text: **Confirmed**: these two `@crustjs/skills` failures are PRE-EXISTING on the orch branch (HEAD~1, before merge) — not introduced by the TP-016 merge. They appear to be environment-related E2E tests that don't pass cleanly in this worktree. Let me return to the merged HEAD and confirm.

payload.argsPreview: cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && git checkout orch/cyan-20260503T045512 2>&1 | head -3 && git log --oneline -2 && git rev-parse HEAD

payload.text: Good, we're back on the merge commit `69039ad`. Let me look at one of the failing tests to confirm it's environmental.

payload.argsPreview: cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge/packages/skills && bun test 2>&1 | grep -A 15 "includes command-reference workflow" | head -40

payload.text:
Confirmed — these are pre-existing wording-mismatch failures in `@crustjs/skills` E2E tests on `orch/cyan-20260503T045512`. The test expects `"Use this skill when..."` but actual output starts with `"You should use this skill when..."`. Completely unrelated to TP-016 (which only touches command aliases in core/plugins/man/docs).

Per the merger guidance: **"do not report SUCCESS just because the git merge itself was conflict-free"** — but the verification failures are pre-existing baseline issues, not caused by this merge. Let me document this transparently in the result.

Let me also re-confirm TP-016-relevant tests all pass:

payload.argsPreview: cd /home/cyan/dev/github.com/chenxin-yan/crust/.worktrees/cyan-20260503T045512/merge && cd packages/core && bun test 2>&1 | tail -5 && echo "---" && cd ../plugins && bun test 2>&1 | tail -5 && echo "---" && cd ../man && bun test 2>&1 | tail -5

payload.text:
All TP-016-relevant packages (`core`, `plugins`, `man`) pass cleanly: 459 + 106 + 5 = 570 tests, 0 failures.

Now let me write the result file. Given that:
- Merge: clean fast-forward via 'ort' (no conflicts)
- Biome check: pass
- TypeScript check: pass (21/21)
- TP-016 packages: all pass (570/570)
- Pre-existing skills E2E baseline failures: 2 (unrelated to TP-016, present on `orch/cyan-...` before merge)

Per merger guidance about strict verification, I'll mark this as **BUILD_FAILURE** to be honest about test failures, even though they're pre-existing — orchestrator should decide. Actually, re-reading: "If any step fails, the merge is FAILURE. Do not report SUCCESS just because the git merge itself was conflict-free." But also: "If verification fails, revert the merge commit before writing the result."

These failures exist BEFORE the merge — reverting won't help and reverting punishes a clean lane for orch-baseline rot. The right call is to keep the merge (TP-016 is genuinely good), but accurately report verification status. Let me check the merger guidance once more — it says revert on failure. Given the strict instruction, I'll revert and report the situation honestly so the orchestrator can investigate the baseline failures.

Wait — reverting will erase TP-016 from orch entirely, which is wrong because the failures aren't caused by TP-016. Let me re-read: "If verification fails, revert the merge commit before writing the result." This is a hard rule. But it would be foolish to apply it when the failures predate the merge.

Let me ask the user to make the call given this nuanced situation.

payload.argsPreview: Verification has 2 pre-existing test failures unrelated to TP-016. How should I proceed?
